content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 26, 2016

John Bakker
Dublin City Attorney
Meyers – Nave
555 12th Street, Suite 1500
Oakland, CA 94607

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-16-062

Dear Mr. Bakker:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding conflict of interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your question is general in nature and not limited to a specific governmental decision, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

QUESTION

Is the City of Dublin responsible for identifying consultants who must file statements of economic interests pursuant to the City's conflict of interest code?

CONCLUSION

The City of Dublin is responsible for ensuring that its conflict of interest code designates all public officials who make or participate in making decisions including consultants and that the disclosure categories are tailored to the duties performed by the designated positions. Moreover, the filing officer is required to determine whether required statements of economic interests have been filed and notify promptly all persons who have failed to file a statement.

FACTS

You are the City Attorney for the City of Dublin (the "City"). In an effort to comply with the Act and the FPPC's regulations governing the economic disclosure process, the City is seeking advice as to determine its responsibility, if any, with respect to identifying the individuals who are "consultants" as defined by Regulation 18700.3. After a thorough review of FPPC regulations and

________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-16-062
Page No. 2

relevant sections of the California Government Code, the City is unclear whether it has an
affirmative duty to identify individual "consultants" who must file economic disclosure statements
(Form 700s) or whether the duty to identify one as a "consultant" lies with the individuals providing
services pursuant to contracts.

The City's conflict of interest code (Dublin Municipal Code Chapter 2.24) identifies a
number of positions that are required to file economic disclosure statements. Among the positions
listed by the City's conflict of interest code are "consultants." Section 2.24.015 indicates that the
definitions in the Act and FPPC regulations apply to terms used in the City's conflict of interest
code. Therefore, the term consultant as used in 2.24.020 has the same meaning as use in the Act
and FPPC regulations.

You stated that the City has 115 active consulting contracts, and it would require substantial
public resources to determine whether individuals performing services under the contracts are
"consultants" under Regulation 18700.3.

ANALYSIS

Section 87300 requires every agency to "adopt and promulgate a conflict of interest code"
designating all "positions within the agency, other than those specified in Section 87200, which
involve the making or participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a
material effect on any financial interest," and (2) identifying those interests of the designated
employees that are reportable.

For each agency conflict of interest code, the Act designates a "code reviewing body." A
code reviewing body is charged with reviewing and approving an agency's conflict of interest code.
Section 87309 further provides that:

"No conflict of interest code or amendment shall be approved by the code
reviewing body or upheld by a court if it:

"(a) Fails to provide reasonable assurance that all foreseeable potential
conflict of interest situations will be disclosed or prevented;

"(b) Fails to provide to each affected person a clear and specific statement of
his duties under the Code; or

"(c) Fails to adequately differentiate between designated employees with
different powers and responsibilities." (Emphasis added.)

Persons in positions designated in an agency's conflict of interest code are known as
"designated employees," a term that includes officers and employees of an agency, as well as an
agency's consultants. (Section 82019.)

For purposes of the Act, a "consultant" is defined in Regulation 18700.3(a) as an individual
who, pursuant to a contract with a public agency:

File No. I-16-062
Page No. 3

"(1) Makes a governmental decision whether to:

"(A) Approve a rate, rule, or regulation;

"(B) Adopt or enforce a law;

"(C) Issue, deny, suspend, or revoke any permit, license, application, certificate, approval, order, or similar authorization or entitlement;

"(D) Authorize the agency to enter into, modify, or renew a contract provided it is the type of contract that requires agency approval;

"(E) Grant agency approval to a contract that requires agency approval and to which the agency is a party, or to the specifications for such a contract;

"(F) Grant agency approval to a plan, design, report, study, or similar item;

"(G) Adopt, or grant agency approval of, policies, standards, or guidelines for the agency, or for any subdivision thereof; or

"(2) Serves in a staff capacity with the agency and in that capacity participates in making a governmental decision as defined in Regulation 18704(a) and (b) or performs the same or substantially all the same duties for the agency that would otherwise be performed by an individual holding a position specified in the agency's conflict of interest code under Section 87302."

Making or participating in a decision is described in Regulation 18704:

"(a) Making a Decision. A public official makes a governmental decision if the official authorizes or directs any action, votes, appoints a person, obligates or commits his or her agency to any course of action, or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of his or her agency.

"(b) Participating in a Decision. A public official participates in a governmental decision if the official provides information, an opinion, or a recommendation for the purpose of affecting the decision without significant intervening substantive review."

Pursuant to the Act, an agency must adopt a conflict of interest code that adequately differentiates between designated employees with different powers and responsibilities. Accordingly, an agency has a duty under existing law to take adequate safeguards in adopting its conflict of interest code and reviewing the duties of individuals (including consultants) to ensure that disclosure is tailored to the individual's actual duties.

To comply with the City's duties in adopting a conflict of interest code, we can generally advise that the code must (1) conform the disclosure requirements for each consultant to the consultant's actual range of duties or (2) require all consultants to file under the broadest disclosure

File No. I-16-062
Page No. 4

category unless or until the agency more narrowly tailors disclosure for a particular consultant and
determines in writing that the consultant is hired to perform a range of duties that are limited in
scope. An agency determining who must file as a consultant and the appropriate level of disclosure
may use FPPC Form 805, "Agency Report of Consultants." The Form 700 Reference Pamphlet also
contains helpful information about consultants filing Form 700.

Additionally, the Act requires that the City's filing officer must determine whether required
statements of economic interest have been filed, notify promptly all persons who have failed to file
a statement of economic interest, and report apparent violations to the appropriate agency. (Section
81010(a) and (c); Regulation 18115(a)(2).) However, for purposes of consultants, the pertinent
question is what should the City do to ensure the filing officer can fulfill these duties. In addressing
this question, we offer the following assistance:

1. The City must have a method in place to review current and future City contracts
and identify those contracts that either:

a. Expressly permit the contractor to make decisions as defined in Regulation
18700.3(a)(1) or

b. Clearly require the contractor to make or participate in decisions in a staff capacity
including, but not limited to, contracts requiring the contractor to perform the same or
similar services of an otherwise designated employee and contracts for consulting services
on high level projects for a period exceeding one year.³

2. For the contracts identified, the City has a responsibility to notify each contractor
of the contractor's obligation to file.

3. The filing officer must report an apparent violation to the appropriate agency if
the consultant/contractor fails to file a statement of economic interests after receiving
notice.⁴ As an example, the filing officer should report an apparent violation after notifying
the contractor of the obligation to file if a contract has been identified that expressly permits
a contractor to make decisions or clearly requires the contractor to serve in a staff capacity,
unless the contractor or an employee of the contractor fulfilling the contractor's
responsibilities under the contract files a statement of economic interests as a consultant.

³ We have provided assistance with the identification of agency consultants in a previous request for advice.
Baker Advice Letter, No. A-15-246.

⁴ We note that this advice is not intended to relieve the filing officer of his or her duty to notify a consultant of
the obligation to file a statement of economic interests or report an apparent violation if the filing officer has actual
knowledge of a specific contractor or employee of the contractor who qualifies as a consultant notwithstanding the
City's review of its contracts.

File No. I-16-062
Page No. 5

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

BGL:jgl

Is the City of Dublin responsible for identifying consultants who must file statements of 
economic interests pursuant to the City's conflict of interest code?
sections.conclusion:
The City of Dublin is responsible for ensuring that its conflict of interest code designates all 
public officials who make or participate in making decisions including consultants and that the 
disclosure categories are tailored to the duties performed by the designated positions. Moreover, the 
filing officer is required to determine whether required statements of economic interests have been 
filed and notify promptly all persons who have failed to file a statement.

"(B) Adopt or enforce a law;

certificate, approval, order, or similar authorization or entitlement;

for the agency, or for any subdivision thereof; or 
"(2) Serves in a staff capacity with the agency and in that capacity 
participates in making a governmental decision as defined in Regulation I 8704(a) 
and (b) or performs the same or substantially all the same duties for the agency that

agency's conflict of interest code under Section 87302." 
Making or participating in a decision is described in Regulation 18704:

agreement on behalf of his or her agency.

governmental decision if the official provides information, an opinion, or a 
recommendation for the purpose of affecting the decision without significant

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is the City of Dublin responsible for identifying consultants who must file statements of 
economic interests pursuant to the City's conflict of interest code?

CONCLUSION: The City of Dublin is responsible for ensuring that its conflict of interest code designates all 
public officials who make or participate in making decisions including consultants and that the 
disclosure categories are tailored to the duties performed by the designated positions. Moreover, the 
filing officer is required to determine whether required statements of economic interests have been 
filed and notify promptly all persons who have failed to file a statement.
